pattern: Within any length-k window the recolor cost equals its white count, so a sliding window finds the cheapest window in one pass.
hints: Recoloring a window to all black costs exactly the number of white blocks it currently holds.
Slide a window of width k across the string, maintaining its running count of 'W' characters.
As the window advances, drop the block leaving on the left and add the one entering on the right, tracking the minimum white count seen.

edge_inputs.description: already k consecutive black needs nothing
all white, must recolor k of them
best window is not at the start
a fully black run exists somewhere in the middle

stress.description: random black/white string near the length cap with varied window size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    blocks = ''.join(rng.choice('WB') for _ in range(n))
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [blocks, k]
